questions.q: What was the first national framework of government for the United States?
questions.explanation: The Articles (1781) created a deliberately weak national government.

questions.q: Which was a major weakness of the Articles of Confederation?
options: Congress could not tax effectively or regulate trade
questions.explanation: The national government could not tax, regulate interstate trade, or enforce its decisions.

options: That the army was too large
That the national government was too weak to keep order
questions.explanation: The government could not help put down the uprising, alarming leaders.

questions.q: What did the 1787 Philadelphia Convention ultimately decide to do?
questions.explanation: Delegates met to revise the Articles but wrote a new Constitution instead.

questions.q: Why did Americans first design a weak national government?
options: They feared concentrated power after fighting a monarch
questions.explanation: Having fought a king, they were wary of strong central authority.
